Title: Characterization of the Biosynthetic Gene Cluster for the Ribosomally Synthesized Cyclic Peptide Epichloecyclins in Epichloë festucae
Journal: Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: GA66 (endophyte-free host inoculated with Fl1-derived strains)
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Secreted multicore RiPP precursor peptide; no conserved domain detected
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: GigA propeptide containing four core peptide repeats
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Serves as the precursor polypeptide that is processed into epichloecyclin core peptides
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key required precursor gene; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly encodes the peptide precursor of the target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: aboveground herbage/pseudostem tissue of infected perennial ryegrass
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Disruption abolished production of all epichloecyclins; complementation partially restored production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. gigA is part of a 7.7 kb coexpressed epichloecyclin biosynthetic cluster. 2. Deletion of gigA eliminated detectable epichloecyclins in infected ryegrass. 3. Complementation restored production only partially, indicating genomic context may affect full expression.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Grass-induced/in planta; symbiosis required to activate gig expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected function during endophytic growth in infected ryegrass
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene disruption; complementation; LC-MS metabolite profiling in infected plants
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: in planta symbiosis with perennial ryegrass; glasshouse growth
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: transcriptome sequences used for gene boundary determination; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No gross host phenotype change reported for ΔgigA; negligible influence on other endophyte alkaloids overall
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering or activating epichloecyclin production in endophyte-plant symbioses, but ecological and nutritional value remains unclear
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GigA protein accession ACB30126.1 in Table 1; contains signal peptide and LXKR cleavage motifs; full activity may depend on native genomic context

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: GA66 (endophyte-free host inoculated with Fl1-derived strains)
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Processed linear GigA-derived epichloecyclin peptide intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes/associates with cyclization of GigA-derived core peptides, likely linking conserved tyrosine to the N-terminal residue to form cyclic peptides
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for cyclization; essential for cyclic product formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: aboveground herbage/pseudostem tissue of infected perennial ryegrass
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Unknown ecological role; may act as bioactive molecules
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion abolished all detectable cyclic epichloecyclins; complementation restored production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. gigB encodes a DUF3328 protein required for epichloecyclin cyclization. 2. ΔgigB plants lacked all cyclic epichloecyclins. 3. Complementation restored epichloecyclin accumulation. 4. ΔgigB infection correlated with increased host tiller number, height trend, and biomass.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Grass-induced/in planta; clustered and coregulated with gigA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional only evaluated in infected ryegrass symbiosis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene disruption; complementation; LC-MS metabolite profiling; peptidomics attempted for intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: in planta symbiosis with perennial ryegrass; glasshouse growth
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: transcriptome sequences used for annotation; peptidomics; LC-MS metabolomics; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ΔgigB infection increased host tiller number and biomass; no major fungal morphology defect reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to toggle cyclic peptide production in endophytes, though altered host growth and unknown ecological consequences warrant caution
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Linear intermediates were not detected, likely due to instability; strongest functional evidence supports role in cyclization

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: GA66 (endophyte-free host inoculated with Fl1-derived strains)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes N-methylation, including dimethylation, of the conserved lysine residue in epichloecyclins
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key tailoring step; required for methylated final products
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: aboveground herbage/pseudostem tissue of infected perennial ryegrass
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion abolished methylated epichloecyclins and caused accumulation of desmethylepichloecyclins; complementation restored methylated products partially
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. gigC encodes a SET-domain methyltransferase in the epichloecyclin cluster. 2. ΔgigC plants accumulated desmethylepichloecyclins but lacked methylated epichloecyclins. 3. Complementation restored both methylated and desmethyl forms toward WT-like totals. 4. Methylation can occur late in the pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Grass-induced/in planta; clustered and coregulated with gigA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional only evaluated in infected ryegrass symbiosis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene disruption; complementation; LC-MS profiling of methylated and demethylated metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: in planta symbiosis with perennial ryegrass; glasshouse growth
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: transcriptome sequences used for annotation; LC-MS metabolomics; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No major host phenotype effect except increased tiller number in ΔgigC-infected plants; negligible influence on other alkaloids overall
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful tailoring enzyme for controlling methylation state of fungal cyclic peptides in metabolic engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Partial complementation suggests native genomic context may influence full activity/expression

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: GA66 (endophyte-free host inoculated with Fl1-derived strains)
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: GigA propeptide at kexin recognition sites
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Cleaved peptide intermediates enabling epichloecyclin maturation; specifically required for epichloecyclin A processing
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Proteolytically cleaves GigA propeptide/intermediates at kexin-like recognition sites during maturation of epichloecyclins
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: specialized but partially redundant processing step
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: aboveground herbage/pseudostem tissue of infected perennial ryegrass
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion abolished epichloecyclin A but retained near-WT levels of epichloecyclins B-E, indicating partial redundancy for cleavage; complementation restored epichloecyclin A
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. kexB encodes a subtilisin-like protease in the cluster. 2. ΔkexB retained epichloecyclins B-E but lacked epichloecyclin A, indicating a specialized role in precursor cleavage. 3. Complementation restored full product profile. 4. ΔkexB also caused abnormal fungal morphology in planta.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Grass-induced/in planta; clustered and coregulated with gigA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional only evaluated in infected ryegrass symbiosis
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Golgi apparatus likely discussed for kexin-type processing, not directly proven for KexB
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene disruption; complementation; LC-MS metabolite profiling; confocal microscopy of fungal morphology
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: in planta symbiosis with perennial ryegrass; glasshouse growth
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: transcriptome sequences used for annotation; LC-MS metabolomics; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ΔkexB caused abnormal in planta hyphal growth and reduced lolitrem B/F in host tissue; endophyte biomass trended lower
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for selective editing of peptide processing, but pleiotropic fungal growth effects make it a cautious engineering target
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion suggests partial redundancy with KexA; epichloecyclin A appears specifically dependent on KexB
